title: Reference-Locked Character Prompt Generator Input
description: Input schema for generating a plain-text image prompt from 1 to 5 attached reference images. The generated prompt is optimized for character identity preservation and character sheet workflows for image models such as GPT Image 2 or Nano Banana. This skill never generates images.

reference_images.description: The attached reference images to use. Provide between 1 and 5 images. Use stable IDs such as @img1, @img2, @img3, @img4, and @img5.

image_ref.description: Reference token for the attached image. Keep this exact token in the generated prompt.

role.description: How this image should be used when generating the prompt.

priority.description: Priority from 1 to 5. Higher means stronger authority when references conflict.

notes.description: Optional human guidance about what this image contributes. Use an empty string if not needed.

target_model.description: The image model or prompt style to optimize for. This skill still returns a plain text prompt only.

output_language.description: Language of the generated prompt. Image reference tokens such as @img1 must remain unchanged.

task_type.description: The kind of image prompt to produce.

style_family.description: Requested visual style. Use auto to let the skill match the attached references and user goal.

realism_level.description: Controls how realistic or stylized the generated prompt should be.

consistency_strength.description: How strongly the prompt should lock the character to the reference images.

prompt_detail_level.description: How detailed the generated prompt should be.

conflict_priority.description: Which source wins if text instructions conflict with reference image appearance.

sheet_options.description: Controls which elements appear in the character sheet prompt.

pose_variations: standing naturally with clear identity lock
looking directly at the camera while walking forward
interacting with the environment while preserving outfit and face identity

pose_variations.description: Full-body pose variations to include. These can be localized by the skill in the output language.

detail_callouts.description: Visual details to isolate in close-up callouts.

character_preservation.description: Visual elements that must be preserved from the references.

negative_constraints.description: Additional negative requirements to include in the final prompt.
default: auto

custom_instructions.description: Extra instructions to blend into the prompt. Use an empty string if not needed.

action_category.title: Action category
action_category.description: Choose which action-pose category should drive the pose variations. Use mixed to combine all categories.

action_selection_mode.title: Action selection mode
action_selection_mode.description: Use random to let the app choose action prompts from the selected category each run. Use custom_only to use only custom action prompts.

action_count.title: Number of random actions
action_count.description: How many action prompts to select for the generated sheet.

posing_eye_contact_actions.title: Posing & Eye Contact actions
posing_eye_contact_actions.description: Pose and gaze options for direct eye contact, glances, standing poses, and camera-aware character presentation.
default: looking directly at the camera while walking forward
three-quarter stance with confident eye contact
over-the-shoulder glance while turning back
standing in a relaxed pose with soft eye contact
chin slightly raised with a calm focused gaze
side profile pose with eyes looking toward the viewer

movement_dynamics_actions.title: Movement & Dynamics actions
movement_dynamics_actions.description: Motion-heavy action options such as running, jumping, spinning, lunging, dancing, and wind-swept movement.
default: running toward the camera with natural motion
jumping mid-air with hair and clothing moving naturally
spinning gracefully with dynamic body flow
stepping forward with wind-swept motion
kicking or lunging in a controlled action pose
dancing with elegant full-body movement

emotional_actions.title: Emotional Actions
emotional_actions.description: Body-language actions that communicate mood, confidence, surprise, shyness, determination, or playfulness.
default: smiling brightly with an open welcoming gesture
serious determined stance with clenched focus
surprised reaction pose with expressive hands
shy gentle pose with softened shoulders
confident heroic stance with strong posture
playful laughing pose with relaxed body language

interaction_actions.title: Interactions
interaction_actions.description: Actions where the character interacts with props, surfaces, light, wind, or the surrounding environment while preserving identity.
default: leaning against a nearby wall or railing
holding a relevant prop without changing the outfit identity
reaching toward an object in the environment
walking through the environment while interacting with light and wind
resting one hand on a surface while keeping full body visible
using the environment as framing while preserving the character identity

custom_action_prompts.title: Custom action prompts
custom_action_prompts.description: Optional custom poses or actions. Leave empty to use the built-in randomized action pools.

choreography_preset.title: Choreography sheet preset
choreography_preset.description: Optional preset for a professional dance-sequence instruction sheet. Use kpop_4x4_instruction_sheet for a 16-frame K-pop choreography diagram based on the attached reference image.

choreography_grid_layout.title: Choreography grid layout
choreography_grid_layout.description: Panel grid for the choreography sequence sheet.

choreography_frame_count.title: Choreography frame count
choreography_frame_count.description: Number of numbered dance-step frames to request. Use 16 for a 4x4 K-pop instruction sheet.

choreography_visual_style.title: Choreography visual style
choreography_visual_style.description: Rendering and layout style for the dance instruction sheet.

choreography_wardrobe_mode.title: Choreography wardrobe mode
choreography_wardrobe_mode.description: How wardrobe should be handled while preserving the attached reference identity.

choreography_motion_indicators.title: Motion indicators
choreography_motion_indicators.description: Instructional overlays to show movement direction, rhythm, weight transfer, turns, and body isolation.
default: curved arrows for flowing movement
straight arrows for directional steps
circular indicators for spins and turns
weight-transfer markers
body-isolation guide lines

choreography_panel_structure.title: Panel structure
choreography_panel_structure.description: What each choreography frame should contain.
choreography_panel_structure.default: step number and short move name at top-left; full-body pose centered; 3-4 concise instruction lines at bottom-left; motion arrows and direction guides overlay each panel

choreography_restrictions.title: Choreography restrictions
choreography_restrictions.description: Restrictions for the choreography sheet. These keep the output clean and locked to the attached reference character.
default: use the attached reference image as the base character identity
same female dancer across every panel
preserve face likeness and body proportions from the reference image
clean white background
thin black panel dividers
no color
no background scene
no extra characters
no clutter
only dancer and instruction elements
